meaning: n.摆动,鼓翼,烦扰vi.鼓翼,飘动,悸动,乱跳,烦扰vt.拍(翅),使焦急
examples: Her heart gave a flutter when she saw him.
The sound of his voice in the hall made her heart flutter.
Her sudden arrival caused quite a flutter.
Who else would risk a flutter on one of the world's most unpredictable elections?
"I don't usually bet but it might be worth a flutter, " he told me.
